description:
You are a level-1 support engineer who has just been handed a “support case” directory sitting at:

        /home/user/support_case/

    At ticket hand-off time this directory already contains two small configuration files:

        1. /home/user/support_case/app_config.yaml
        2. /home/user/support_case/system_info.toml

    Your assignment consists of exactly three high-level steps that can be completed with only a couple of shell commands:

        STEP 1  (app_config.yaml)
            • In the YAML file, switch the service from “disabled” to “enabled”.
            • Also raise the logging level from “info” to “debug”.
            • Do NOT touch anything else in that YAML file (spacing, keys, comments, etc.).

        STEP 2  (system_info.toml)
            • Add a brand-new TOML table named [network] as the very last block in the file.
            • Inside that table insert these two key/value pairs exactly:
                    ip = "192.168.1.50"
                    up = true
              Preserve the existing TOML formatting for the other tables.

        STEP 3  (diag_report.log)
            • After updating the two config files, create a plain-text report file called
                    /home/user/support_case/diag_report.log
            • The report must contain exactly four lines, no leading or trailing whitespace, no blank lines.
            • Each line must be a single key followed by a colon and the value, with NO embedded spaces.
                  The required keys and the values they must reflect are:

                  Line 1 → SERVICE_STATUS:<value of service.enabled in YAML, e.g. true>
                  Line 2 → LOG_LEVEL:<value of logging.level in YAML, e.g. debug>
                  Line 3 → NETWORK_IP:<value of network.ip in TOML, e.g. 192.168.1.50>
                  Line 4 → NETWORK_UP:<value of network.up in TOML, e.g. true>

              Example of the exact layout that the automated grader will parse (do not copy literal values—use the ones produced by your edits):

                  SERVICE_STATUS:true
                  LOG_LEVEL:debug
                  NETWORK_IP:192.168.1.50
                  NETWORK_UP:true

    Constraints & Hints
    -------------------
    • You do NOT need to install extra software; standard shell utilities are enough.  
    • Accomplish the whole task with roughly two or three well-chosen shell commands.  
    • Make sure all edits are flushed to disk before the session ends—open-in-place edits are fine.  
    • All work must stay inside /home/user/support_case/; do not move or rename files.

    Success criteria
    ----------------
    The automated tests will verify:

        1. app_config.yaml now has “enabled: true” and “level: "debug"”.
        2. system_info.toml ends with the new [network] table containing the specified ip and up keys.
        3. /home/user/support_case/diag_report.log exists and its four lines exactly match the pattern described above.

    Good luck, engineer—get those diagnostics collected!
truth:
# ---------- Pre-existing files before the agent begins ----------
    # 1. YAML
    Path: /home/user/support_case/app_config.yaml
    Original contents (including spacing):
        service:
          name: "FileProcessor"
          version: "1.2.3"
          enabled: false

        logging:
          level: "info"
          path: "/var/log/fileprocessor/"

    # 2. TOML
    Path: /home/user/support_case/system_info.toml
    Original contents:
        [cpu]
        cores = 4
        model = "Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8250U"

        [memory]
        total_gb = 8

        [os]
        name = "Ubuntu"
        version = "20.04"

    # ---------- Expected state after correct agent execution ----------
    # Updated YAML
    Path: /home/user/support_case/app_config.yaml
    Expected contents:
        service:
          name: "FileProcessor"
          version: "1.2.3"
          enabled: true

        logging:
          level: "debug"
          path: "/var/log/fileprocessor/"

    # Updated TOML
    Path: /home/user/support_case/system_info.toml
    Expected contents:
        [cpu]
        cores = 4
        model = "Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8250U"

        [memory]
        total_gb = 8

        [os]
        name = "Ubuntu"
        version = "20.04"

        [network]
        ip = "192.168.1.50"
        up = true

    # Diagnostic report
    Path: /home/user/support_case/diag_report.log
    Expected exact contents (4 lines, UNIX newline at EOF):
        SERVICE_STATUS:true
        LOG_LEVEL:debug
        NETWORK_IP:192.168.1.50
        NETWORK_UP:true
name: task_000000_fa59d6d2
